title.description: Concise title for this session, like a git commit subject. One phrase, no period. Under 80 characters.

summary.description: 1-2 sentences, under 40 words. First sentence: what was accomplished (not how). Second sentence: the single most important challenge or blocker — omit if straightforward.

outcome.description: Goal-relative outcome. 'completed' = goal achieved (tests pass, question answered, plan written). 'progressed' = meaningful progress, not done. 'abandoned' = stopped without resolution. 'pivoted' = changed direction mid-session.

outcome_confidence.description: How confident are you in the outcome classification? 'low' if intent is unclear or session is very short.

session_types.description: What kind of work this session involved. Use multiple values if the session pivoted (e.g. debugging then implementation).

topics.description: Topic tags in 'category:tag' format. Pick from the provided vocabulary when possible, mint new tags within a category when nothing fits. 1-8 tags.

category.description: tool_failure: tool errors, retries, crashes. user_correction: user corrected the assistant's approach. external_blocker: rate limits, missing permissions, network issues. workflow_antipattern: inefficient patterns like sequential reads, unnecessary re-reads, not checking --help.

description.description: Concise description of the frustration. One sentence.

severity.description: minor: brief annoyance, recovered quickly. significant: wasted multiple turns or required backtracking. blocking: prevented progress on the main goal.

frustrations.description: Categorized frustrations detected in the session. Empty array if none.

actionable_insight.description: One actionable observation for future sessions. Must be something concrete that could improve workflow next time. Null if nothing novel or actionable.

is_research_subagent.description: True if this session is a subagent doing research/exploration/evaluation work.

research_topic.description: If is_research_subagent is true, the topic being researched. Null otherwise.

research_tags.description: If is_research_subagent is true, searchable tags for the research. Null otherwise.
